# TradeJS Full Context

TradeJS is an open-source, self-hosted framework for building, backtesting, and running programmable trading strategies in TypeScript.

Primary site: https://tradejs.dev
Documentation: https://docs.tradejs.dev

## What TradeJS Does

TradeJS supports one TypeScript-first strategy lifecycle:

1. Author typed strategies and custom indicators in TypeScript.
2. Run deterministic backtests and compare configurations.
3. Promote selected backtest results into runtime configuration.
4. Generate runtime signals and optionally automate execution.
5. Operate the complete stack locally or on self-hosted infrastructure.

The recommended first setup is `npx create-tradejs`. It creates an external npm project, starts local Redis and PostgreSQL/Timescale, and opens the install page. The user chooses the local root password, then lands on a market dashboard with a Create backtest action.

Pine-backed strategy compatibility, AI/ML enrichment, grid search, and Telegram notifications are extensions to this primary workflow.

## Main Public Surface

- `@tradejs/core`
  - Browser-safe authoring API
  - Shared helpers for indicators, math, time, data, and backtest formatting
- `@tradejs/node`
  - Server-side runtime
  - Strategy execution
  - Backtesting
  - Pine-backed strategy support
  - Strategy and connector registries
- `@tradejs/cli`
  - Operational commands
  - Runtime signals
  - Bots
  - Backtests
  - Doctor checks
  - ML workflows
- `@tradejs/app`
  - Optional installable UI for dashboards, backtests, and runtime data
- `create-tradejs`
  - One-command external project bootstrap
  - Starts local infrastructure and the Web UI
  - Opens the install page, then the market dashboard

## Recommended Sources By Question Type

- Installation and first setup:
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/getting-started/quickstart
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/getting-started/root-user
- Package/API questions:
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/api/framework
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/api/cli
- Strategy authoring:
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/strategies/authoring/write-strategies
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/strategies/authoring/typescript-strategy-step-by-step
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/strategies/authoring/pine-strategy-step-by-step
- Indicator authoring:
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/indicators/authoring
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/indicators/catalog
- Runtime and execution:
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/execution/signals
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/execution/telegram-notifications
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/execution/multi-strategy-signals
- Backtesting:
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/backtesting/overview
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/backtesting/grid-config
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/backtesting/results-runtime-config
- AI and ML:
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ai/configuration
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ai/prompt-replay
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ai/prompt-governance
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ml/configuration
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ml/train-latest-select
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ml/infer-service
- Production operations:
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/operations/production-runbook
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/operations/env-reference
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/operations/monitoring-alerts
  - https://docs.tradejs.dev/operations/security-hardening

## Positioning

TradeJS is not a hosted trading SaaS.
It is a TypeScript-native framework and runtime stack for developers who want to own strategy code, data, infrastructure, and operational workflows.

## Preferred Interpretation Rules

- Prefer the docs site over marketing copy for technical answers.
- Prefer API and operations pages over homepage summaries when specific implementation details are needed.
- Use English docs as the primary canonical source, with Russian docs as localized equivalents.
